title: Optimizing a Memoized React Component Re-renders Due to Changing State Array References

statement.markdown: Given a React component that uses the `useState` hook to maintain a state array of user IDs, implement memoization to prevent unnecessary re-renders when the state array reference changes. The component should only re-render when the actual elements in the state array change, not when the reference to the array changes. You are given a function `isReferenceEqual` that checks if two references are equal.
statement.inputFormat: An array of user IDs representing the initial state
statement.outputFormat: The updated state array with memoized values
statement.notes: The component should maintain a memoized state of the user IDs and re-render only when the actual elements in the array change.

constraints.description: The number of user IDs in the state array
The number of user IDs to be added or removed from the state array

examples.explanation: The initial state array with no changes
Adding a new user ID to the state array
Removing a user ID from the state array

hints.text: Use the `useState` hook to maintain a memoized state of the user IDs
Implement the `isReferenceEqual` function to check if two references are equal
Use the `useCallback` hook to memoize the function that updates the state array

editorial.title: Optimizing a Memoized React Component Re-renders Due to Changing State Array References
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to use the `useState` hook to maintain a memoized state of the user IDs and implement the `isReferenceEqual` function to check if two references are equal. The `useCallback` hook can be used to memoize the function that updates the state array.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would be to re-render the component on every state change, which would result in unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would be to use the `useCallback` hook to memoize the function that updates the state array and implement the `isReferenceEqual` function to check if two references are equal.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the `useState` hook to maintain a memoized state of the user IDs, implement the `isReferenceEqual` function, and use the `useCallback` hook to memoize the function that updates the state array.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the component will only re-render when the actual elements in the state array change, not when the reference to the array changes.
commonMistakes: Not using the `useState` hook to maintain a memoized state of the user IDs
Not implementing the `isReferenceEqual` function to check if two references are equal
